newsSinceJun6: No company-specific fundamental news since June 6. The stock fell ~7% on June 8 in a sector-wide AI/semiconductor sell-off (SOX-led), then rebounded +3.5% today on no new information - a flow-driven bounce, not a fundamental event. The supportive fundamentals are pre-window: mid-May FY6/26 order guidance raised to JPY200-240B from JPY170-220B on AI-driven EUV inspection demand (ACTIS A200HiT), and Goldman Sachs reaffirmed Buy on June 1.

sources.label: Futu News - Lasertec revises order outlook upward on AI-driven EUV inspection demand
Yahoo Finance Japan - FY6/26 order guidance range upward revision (Toushiru)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new fundamental evidence since June 6; today's +3.5% is a flow-driven rebound from the June 8 sector sell-off, and yesterday's hold already reflected the post-selloff setup. The May order-book raise that underpins the WFE thesis is already in the score.
verdicts.bull: FY6/26 order guidance was raised to JPY200-240B on AI semiconductor demand, and actinic EUV mask inspection remains a near-monopoly tollgate on every advanced-node and HBM-adjacent litho capacity addition.
verdicts.neutral: Stock chopped -7% then +3.5% on zero company news; net of the round trip it sits ~4% below its June 5 level with the order thesis intact but no incremental datapoint to act on.
verdicts.bear: 27.7% YTD with a $23B cap means the order-revision is largely priced; order timing lumpiness and China export-control exposure can still vaporize a quarter of bookings.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY6/26 (June year-end) results and FY27 order guidance, plus ACTIS A200HiT order announcements tied to 2nm/A16 EUV mask volume.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Order push-outs or export-control tightening hitting the China-linked portion of the book; semicap sector beta as shown by the June 8 SOX-led drawdown.

verdicts.rankRationale: On this crowding-sensitive alpha list, a +3.5% no-news rebound that claws back the June 8 discount marginally reduces residual asymmetry, and Lasertec is a heavily-covered former retail darling rather than a hidden name. Nothing new enough since yesterday's hold to justify a rank change, so hold with a small negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Actinic mask inspection is genuinely scarce - effectively sole-source - and the raised JPY200-240B order outlook shows AI/EUV demand pulling through despite the broader semicap chop.
verdicts.neutral: The mask/yield-control thesis is intact but indirect to CPO; this week's price action was pure sector beta and the alpha case is unchanged from June 8.
verdicts.bear: With 9 analysts on it, a Goldman Buy reaffirm, and 27.7% YTD, ownership and awareness are high; semicap spending volatility plus discovery already done leaves limited under-owned upside on an alpha lens.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: New actinic inspection tool orders or an FY27 order outlook above the raised JPY200-240B range at the June fiscal-year-end report.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded positioning unwinding into any order miss - the June 8 -7% sector flush shows how fast the multiple compresses on this name.
